Torna Idioma — Workshop Demo

Método Rizal · Vida · Cultura · Legado
An AI that teaches you to SPEAK Spanish
Login
demo@tornaidioma.ph
Password
TornaDemo2026
The one-line pitch: Most apps make you read and tap. Torna Idioma places you at your level, then has you listen, speak, and converse with an AI tutor that corrects you — and gives you a real, can-be-failed score. The big demo moment is Step 3: you talk, Isabel talks back.
The 8-minute demo flow

Sign in

Open tornaidioma.com → tap Experience the Platform (or go to /Torna_Idioma/login) and sign in with the account above. The app opens in Tagalog by default.

Placement Test — "we start you at the right level"

Left menu → Placement Test. Answer a few multiple-choice questions, then the spoken part (tap the mic or type). It returns an honest CEFR level (A1/A2/B1) and a recommended first lesson.

Point to make: the spoken part can only lower an over-confident score — readers who can't talk don't get over-placed. That's real assessment.

Speak Now — the speaking lesson (the WOW)

Left menu → Speak Now → open Presentaciones. Walk the 5 steps:

1 Listen — tap ▶ to hear the conversation. 2 Shadow — play a line, then repeat it out loud. 3 Speak — say the phrase; Isabel scores you and gives a tip. 4 Converse — have a real voice chat with Isabel (she plays a stranger at a Makati event). 5 Assessment — introduce yourself; get a real rubric score.

Say into the mic: "Hola, me llamo [your name]. Soy de Manila. ¿Cómo te llamas?" — Isabel replies out loud and corrects you.

Show a real score (not auto-100)

On Step 5 Assessment, give a short/weak answer on purpose. The score comes back with five dimensions (fluency, accuracy, pronunciation, range, interaction) and can say "Not yet." Then point out: this certificate can be earned AND failed.

Mistakes to review — "it remembers what you got wrong"

Go back to Speak Now. The Mistakes to review panel lists what you missed, with the correct version (tap ▶ to hear it) and a "Got it" button. Completed lessons show a green ✓ and your best score.

Quick tour of the rest

AI Spanish Tutor — free conversation + "Día de Español" immersion. Cinco Raíces — five root words a night (José Rizal's method). Rizal Studies — learn through Rizal's own Spanish works. Emperador — streaks, badges, leaderboard.

The bilingual flip (closing wow)

Top-left, switch FIL → EN (or back). The entire interface changes language instantly — Tagalog by default for Filipino learners, English on tap. The Spanish you're learning always stays Spanish.

Before you present — 4 quick tips

  • Allow the microphone once (tap the mic, accept the prompt) before the room is watching.
  • On iPad/Safari the mic can be flaky — every speaking box also accepts typed answers, so the demo never stalls.
  • Install it: on iPad, Share → Add to Home Screen → it opens full-screen like a native app (great first impression).
  • Best internet you can get — the AI voice + scoring are live calls (about a second each).
